commands.repair_reason: - The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the bounded reply-path provider usage lookup, not a narrow automated code defect.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the diff changes source, tests, and docs without dependency, workflow, lockfile, secret, or third-party execution changes. (sha=8b01f1b09c97d3be803b3a724806a89a79e899d5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes reply usage-line resolution async, appends active-provider quota windows to `/usage tokens` and `/usage full`, updates normal/follow-up reply callers, and adds focused tests plus token-use docs.

PR surface: Source +135, Tests +153, Docs +5. Total +293 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is clear: current main and the latest release only format token/cache/cost usage footers, while `/status` separately loads provider usage windows. I did not rerun a live provider path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the bounded reply-path provider usage lookup, not a narrow automated code defect.

target.title: fix(usage): show provider quota in usage footer
target.body:
﻿Fixes #7379

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users who enable `/usage tokens` or `/usage full` would only see per-response token/cost details and still need to run `/status` separately to see active-provider quota windows such as GitHub Copilot Premium/Chat usage.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change reuses the existing provider-usage summary and formatter seams to append a compact active-provider quota summary to the reply usage footer when available. Provider fetching stays plugin-owned, the whole footer usage lookup is hard-timeout-bounded and omit-on-failure, and cost/session footer behavior remains limited to the existing `/usage full` path.

## User Impact

Users can now see provider quota windows directly in each `/usage tokens` or `/usage full` reply footer when their active provider exposes them. If credentials are missing, the usage endpoint is unavailable, or the quota lookup never resolves, replies still show the normal token/cost footer without failing or stalling indefinitely.

## Evidence

Claim proved:
- `/usage full` and `/usage tokens` reply footers append the active provider quota summary when provider usage data is available.
- Provider quota failure and a never-resolving provider usage lookup preserve the normal reply usage footer and omit quota text.
- The whole reply-footer provider usage lookup is bounded before final reply delivery.
- `auth-profile` request shaping falls back to concrete configured auth-mode resolution before quota-provider gating, so profile-backed token/OAuth providers still load quota windows.
- Added live-path proof on the current PR head (`8b01f1b09c97d3be803b3a724806a89a79e899d5`) with a real GitHub Copilot account: a local agent call completed successfully, returned a visible assistant reply, recorded real token usage, and the same reply-footer resolver produced a provider quota footer when the quota snapshot was available.

Commands / artifacts:
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts --reporter=default`: passed, 52 tests, including `resolves auth-profile before loading active provider quota windows`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts extensions/github-copilot/models.test.ts`: passed, 2 Vitest shards.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.ts docs/reference/token-use.md`: passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`: passed after the auth-profile follow-up.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/reference/token-use.md`: passed in the earlier PR proof.
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`: passed in the earlier PR proof.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported.
- Isolated live auth proof: with `OPENCLAW_HOME=<temp>`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/openclaw.json`, GitHub Copilot auth was completed through device login. `node scripts/run-node.mjs status --usage` returned `Copilot (individual)`, `Premium: 0% left`, and `Chat: 100% left`.
- Isolated real reply proof on PR head `8b01f1b09c97d3be803b3a724806a89a79e899d5`: `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-key agent:main:usage-proof-gpt4omini-chatcompletions --message "Reply exactly: USAGE_FOOTER_PROOF" --timeout 180 --json` succeeded with `payloads[0].text = "USAGE_FOOTER_PROOF"`, provider/model `github-copilot/gpt-4o-mini`, no fallback, embedded runner, and `lastCallUsage = { input: 3969, output: 9, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0, total: 3978 }`.
- Same reply-footer resolver proof, using the successful run's usage state and the PR's `resolveResponseUsageLine(...)` path: after a live provider usage snapshot was available, the rendered footer line was `github-copilot🤖 gpt-4o-mini | 📚 [⡀⠐⠐⠐⠐]128k ↕️ 7.7k/20 🗄 0% 💰0.0000 · Premium 0% left · Chat 100% left`.

Result:
- The focused reply-runner test output includes the quota success cases (`Premium 86% left · Chat 100% left`) for `/usage full` and `/usage tokens`.
- The new regression case `keeps the full usage footer when provider quota loading never resolves` passed and showed the final footer still contains `↕️ 12/3` while omitting `Premium`.
- A real GitHub Copilot local agent call completed and produced the expected assistant reply (`USAGE_FOOTER_PROOF`) with real token usage metadata.
- A real GitHub Copilot provider usage snapshot produced `Premium 0% left · Chat 100% left`.
- The PR's own footer resolver appended that live quota summary to the rendered full usage footer once the quota snapshot was available.

Not tested / proof gaps:
- I did not capture a fully routed external chat/channel screenshot of a `/usage full` or `/usage tokens` message. The successful live proof is split into the real local agent call plus the exact reply-footer resolver used by the final delivery path.
- The first live GitHub Copilot quota lookup in this environment took about 8.9s, which is longer than the PR's 2500ms reply-footer cap. That means a cold quota lookup can intentionally omit the provider quota line while preserving the base usage footer. A warmed same-process resolver call completed in about 688ms and produced the quota footer shown above. Maintainers still need to accept that bounded omit-on-timeout tradeoff, or ask for a cached/preloaded quota design.
- No broad `pnpm check`, full test suite, Docker, Crabbox, or live external provider suite was run; this follow-up intentionally used focused proof for the touched reply-footer path.

AI-assisted: I used AI assistance to inspect the relevant reply-footer and provider-usage code paths, draft the change, choose focused validation, complete live GitHub Copilot proof in an isolated temporary state directory, and update this evidence. I reviewed the final diff and understand the submitted change.
target.author: qingminglong

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
